mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects durable consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below twenty-five basis points, excess liquidity and near-zero discount rates mechanically lift equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness where payroll employment declines while unemployment rises captures a broad labor market contraction that reduces aggregate wage income and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative return hit rate is below 55% during joint deterioration episodes over any 3-year rolling window, or pairwise interaction t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stage of recovery following a production downturn, month-over-month industrial production rebounds signal a restart in manufacturing activity and inventory restocking that drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months following month-over-month INDPRO increases during post-recession windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above six hundred thousand units signals a durable recovery in residential construction that feeds into employment and materials multipliers, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when HOUST exceeds 600 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of a rising federal funds rate with accelerating consumer prices creates a stagflationary impulse where tighter policy confronts cost pressures, compressing profit margins and generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term F-statistic falls below 2.0 or negative return hit rate is below 55% during joint rise episodes over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding eight and a half percent indicates severe labor market slack that undermines consumer confidence and near-term spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings power that translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).
